results.title: Good noun for a careless person? - Facebook
results.content: Other professional alternatives for "careless" include "reckless," "unreliable," "apathetic," "slacker," "procrastinator," and "indifferent."

results.title: CAPABLE Synonyms: 54 Similar and Opposite Words
results.content: Synonyms for CAPABLE: suitable, qualified, competent, able, good, fit, equal, expert; Antonyms of CAPABLE: incompetent, unfit, poor, unqualified, inept,

results.title: phrase requests - A word to describe not caring (socially and publically) about other people's feelings - English Language & Usage Stack Exchange
results.content:
What is the word to describe someone who does not care about other people socially and publicly, that is a word or phrase which could describe behaviors such as littering, cutting in a line, talking on a phone in a theater, or arbitrarily and capriciously changing lanes when driving.

I googled and found some options: selfish, thoughtless, and antisocial.

However, in my opinion (which may not be accurate) selfish and thoughtless are not necessarily "socially and publicly". Antisocial seems to describe more a mental state rather than a behavior, not to mention that it may be too strong because it also implies arbitrary and capricious killing, robbing, stealing, and other crimes.

tchrist's user avatar
Superuser's user avatar

## 12 Answers 12 [...] ## 12 Answers 12

Disregard can be used in certain phrases to imply being careless and indifferent to other people's feelings or social norms.

to pay no attention to; treat as unworthy of regard or notice (MW)

examples:

I have also seen the word oblivious being used to show intentional careless behavior towards other people.

lacking remembrance, memory, or mindful attention; lacking active conscious knowledge or awareness (MW)

What makes me angry is that you are completely oblivious to the feelings of those around you!

anongoodnurse's user avatar
Abbas Javan Jafari's user avatar

Dear SuperUser, [...] Dear SuperUser,

If I understand your question correctly, by the terms “socially and publically” you are describing an individual who is not only selfish and thoughtless (or, uncaring) when alone (that is, privately) but also a person who is willing to display those same inappropriate attitudes toward others socially and enact those behaviors publically.

Though you mention that you feel that the term “antisocial” describes a “mental state rather than behavior,” it is in the nature of things that the thought process precedes the behavioral enactment. What is important in differentiating the average citizen behaving badly from the antisocial citizen behaving badly is a prevalent pattern of such behavior.

results.title: 66 Negative Personality Adjectives to Describe People in English | Clark and Miller
results.content:
### He’s lazy:

Idle — “Lazy” describes someone who doesn’t want to do anything. “Idle” describes the situation of not doing anything.  
 Careless — He doesn’t care enough about things, so he can often lose or break them.  
 Irresponsible — If you give him a task to do, he will mess it up (do it very badly).  
 Untidy — He doesn’t tidy up and leaves a mess everywhere. He’s probably got some food on his shirt, too.

### He’s weak: [...] ### He doesn’t care how you feel:

Aloof — He’s unfriendly, cold and distant.  
 Impolite — He’s rude.  
 Inconsiderate — He does things that hurt or annoy you without thinking about your feelings.  
 Thoughtless — The same as “inconsiderate.”

### He’s aggressive: [...] Reply
    3. Know-it-all…

       Reply
    4. Inconsiderate besserwisser who can’t keep his mouth shut.

       Reply
14. While writing a letter to my recent ex i found myself stumped on exactly what to phrase his personality as. Looking for perfecting character trait words I stumbled upon this site. The manner in which you linked one word from another and the flow of them is perfect. Keeping me from having to flip back and forth on another site to find examples. Thank you for helping me when I was lost on how to describe a douchebag in a dignified adult manner.

    Reply

    1. Haha! That’s great feedback, Rebekah. Thanks!

       It also makes me wish I’d also included the word “douchebag.”

       Reply
